title: Two way connection on linked board
excerpt:

posts.author: Ammikas (Armin)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi, I have played with one automation for hours but haven’t been able to get it to work.

The automation works like this: When an item is moved to a group on Board A a new item on board B is created and linked with the board A.
The automatic creation of new item works but the problem is when I start to edit the item.
Anything I do on board A is not reflected on board B. It only reflects when I open the linked TAB on board B.

And changes that I do in the linked tab won’t reflect on the item itself.

But I think it should work also by editing any column?

20-12-2022-08-02-301553×588 52 KB

posts.author: tanner_elevate (Tanner Nelson | Elevate Operations)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Ammikas

My first thought is this - are the columns on board B mirror columns? If they aren’t, they won’t change as you update items on board A because they are technically different items.

If they are mirror columns though, there might be something else at play. I’d be happy to set up a time to meet to get a better idea of what you’re looking for. Let me know! tanner@elevateops.io

Thanks!

Tanner

posts.author: mark.anley (Green Llamas Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Ammikas - when you created the connect board column did you set it up as a two-way connection?

Thanks
Mark

posts.author: Ammikas (Armin)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thanks for you reply. Understood but now the question is, how can I get the sum of mirrored columns?
Because mirrored columns do not give the sum at the bottom.

posts.author: Ammikas (Armin)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes, but I already understood the issue

posts.author: mark.anley (Green Llamas Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Ammikas, you can create a Formula Column and just reference the mirror column you are wanting to sum.

Mark
